judges.cotSummary: All tests pass, no security flags, harness impact zero, but 3473 net additions indicate moderate code churn.
judges.finalJustification: The changes fully integrate with the existing architecture, preserve runtime compatibility, avoid new security surfaces, and maintain high test coverage. The code churn is acceptable given the feature scope, and documentation updates support maintainability.
risks: Moderate code churn could increase maintenance effort over time.
Potential for future regressions if new features are added without corresponding tests.
recommendations: Add regression tests for edge cases introduced by the new memory/skill paths.
Monitor performance impact of the 3473 net additions in production.
Document the new memory/skill API changes in the developer guide.

judges.cotSummary: All tests pass, no new security surfaces, modest code changes and clear documentation; architecture aligns with existing memory/skill pathways.
judges.finalJustification: The change introduces 8 memory and 8 skill files with 3473 lines added, all passing the full test suite (295/295). No dangerous permissions, shell execution, or network calls are added. The modifications respect the existing NanoClaw harness and do not affect runtime compatibility. While the added code is sizable, it remains focused and does not introduce heavy dependencies, keeping the system lightweight. Documentation updates improve observability. Minor risks are limited to potential future maintenance overhead.
risks: Potential maintenance burden as the new modules grow.
Future edge‑case inputs not covered by current tests could expose logic gaps.
recommendations: Add targeted unit tests for boundary conditions of the new memory/skill paths.
Monitor runtime performance for any regressions due to the added code volume.
Keep documentation synchronized with any further changes.
